banking.label: Banking & Finance
banking.description: Nigerian banks use SHL-style numerical and verbal reasoning. Speed and accuracy under pressure.
banking.tips: GTBank and Zenith heavily test data tables and percentage change. Practice reading financial tables fast.

consulting.label: Consulting & Strategy
consulting.description: Case-style numerical reasoning plus Watson-Glaser critical thinking. Logical argument evaluation is key.
consulting.tips: McKinsey PST-style: read the data first, then the question. Never calculate until you know what's needed.

oil_gas.label: Oil & Gas
oil_gas.description: Shell and Chevron use SHL OPQ plus numerical reasoning. Dragnet-style is common for junior roles.
oil_gas.tips: Shell uses time-pressured numerical tests. Estimation is key — don't calculate everything exactly.

fmcg.label: FMCG & Consumer Goods
fmcg.description: Unilever and Nestle use Dragnet + SHL verbal. Graduate trainees need strong verbal reasoning.
fmcg.tips: Unilever Digital Interview tests verbal inference. Read passages carefully — never infer beyond the text.

telco.label: Telecoms & Tech
telco.description: MTN Nigeria uses numerical and abstract reasoning. Tech companies lean toward logical and inductive tests.
telco.tips: Pattern recognition is heavily tested at telcos. Practice sequences and abstract shapes daily.

civil_service.label: Civil Service & Government
civil_service.description: CBN and NNPC use comprehensive aptitude tests covering all areas. WAEC-style knowledge tests included.
civil_service.tips: CBN exams are comprehensive. Time management matters — don't get stuck on one question.

graduate_trainee.label: Graduate Trainee (Any)
graduate_trainee.description: Dragnet is used by 50+ Nigerian companies. Master this and you pass most graduate trainee tests.
graduate_trainee.tips: Dragnet tests 4 areas in one sitting: quantitative, verbal, abstract, and speed. Don't spend too long on any one section.
